message:
feat(session): Add many-to-many relationship flush support with LinkTableOp

Implement comprehensive support for flushing many-to-many relationship changes
through link tables, enabling bidirectional associations between models.

sqlmodel-session changes:
- Add `LinkTableOp` enum representing link/unlink operations for association tables
- Add `LinkTableOp::link()` and `LinkTableOp::unlink()` constructor methods
- Add `LinkTableOp::execute()` for executing individual link table operations
- Add `execute_link_table_ops()` helper for batch execution
- Add `Session::flush_related_many()` method for flushing pending link/unlink
  operations tracked via RelatedMany collections
- Export new types in session crate public API

sqlmodel-schema DDL improvements:
- Refactor format_column_def() for cleaner formatting
- Refactor format_unique_constraint() with proper multiline formatting
- Fix MySQL index type comparison to be case-insensitive (BTREE check)
- Improve test assertion formatting for readability

sqlmodel-core/sqlmodel-query refinements:
- Minor formatting and consistency improvements across row.rs, lib.rs
- Eager loading adjustments in eager.rs
- Migration logic refinements in migrate.rs

The LinkTableOp system allows tracking relationship changes in memory and
persisting them in batches, supporting patterns like:

  hero.powers.link(&fly_power);
  hero.powers.unlink(&x_ray_vision);
  session.flush_related_many(cx, &mut [hero], |h| &mut h.powers, |h| h.id.unwrap(), &link_info).await?;

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
